original_abstract: Due to their diverse and potent biological profiles, quinazoline derivatives have become a focal point of intense research in synthetic chemistry and pharmacology. This review provides a comprehensive overview of recent advancements in the synthesis and biological evaluation of these compounds. To offer a clear structural framework, the synthetic methodologies are categorized into five primary approaches: aza-reactions, microwave-assisted synthesis, metal-mediated transformations, ultrasound-promoted reactions, and phase-transfer catalysis. Furthermore, this work examines the various biological activities exhibited by these newly synthesized scaffolds, highlighting their therapeutic potential and significance in drug discovery.
humanized_abstract: Synthetic Studies towards Quinazoline Derivatives (Mini-Review) Quinazoline derivatives as a consequence of their rich and varied biological activities are among the molecules studied extensively in present day synthetic chemistry and pharmacology. The present article is a critical survey of the progress made in this direction in the recent years. The different methods employed for the construction of quinazoline derivatives such as aza-reactions, microwave-assisted synthesis, metal mediated processes, ultrasound assisted reactions and phase-transfer catalysis are grouped under five heads and illustrated with several important studies. The potential biological activities ascertained through studies of newly synthesized molecules have also been discussed alongwith their possible application in drug design.
